title: City
transcript:
[A picture of various apartment buildings.]
[Text written below the picture:]
Shadowed city slumber silently. A second-story suite.
Come craving courtship, selected serendipitously
Crazed copulations, a salacious storm  of continuous coitus.
Spread, straddled, conquered.
Countless crashed suitors strewn carelessly.
Center, silken sheets sensously [sic] caressing soft skin,
Contentedly sleeps your mom.
explanation:
In non-poetical words, the comic describes the reader's mom, in a bed, having sexual intercourse with other people in a building located within a city.

The poem or description alternates between using words that start with C and words that start with S, to achieve an effect resembling . The gentle, romantic tone of the poem is broken by the last two words, Your Mom. This is an example of a  joke, and is phrased accordingly.
This is a good example of subversion of expectation. The humor of this strip is derived by the juxtaposition of lofty poetic words and mature themes being abruptly offset with a base and juvenile "Your Mom" joke. This is similar to and part of a running gag across the following 89: Gravitational Mass, 158: Six Months, and 176: Before Sunrise, in that all of them start off seemingly innocuous, but end in the abrupt quotation of "Your Mom".

The title text further emphasizes this and even more bluntly drives home the punchline for humorous effect. Notably, this may not be used in a derogatory way. If the narrator is using your mom's services (which is very possible, given the 'countless crashed suitors' apparently strewn about the floor) they may be simply stating fact, or even exclaiming it in a positive way, although the latter is a bit of a stretch.
